plan_policy.reasoning_summary: User wants a coherent rough cut for quick review, not a fragmented highlight reel. Task type set to course_review_summary. Segment count adaptive with target 12, each 6-18 seconds, max final duration 240s. Selection prefers semantic boundaries and avoids repetition to ensure logical flow. Output includes timeline, subtitles, and review sheet.

items.title: 缓存的基本概念
items.review_question: 为什么CPU上要放置一个小容量的缓存？
items.transcript_evidence: One solution is to put a little piece of RAM right on the CPU -- called a cache. There isn’t a lot of space on a processor’s chip, so most caches are just kilobytes or maybe megabytes in size, where RAM is usually gigabytes.
items.selection_reason: Policy repair kept concise block block_0017.

items.title: 缓存加速的原理
items.review_question: 当CPU请求内存时，RAM如何传输数据以利用缓存？
items.transcript_evidence: Having a cache speeds things up in a clever way. When the CPU requests a memory location from RAM, the RAM can transmit not just one single value, but a whole block of data.
items.selection_reason: Policy repair kept concise block block_0018.

items.title: 数据块传输的实用性
items.review_question: 为什么传输一个数据块比只传输单个值更有效？
items.transcript_evidence: This takes only a little bit more time than transmitting a single value, but it allows this data block to be saved into the cache. This tends to be really useful because computer data is often arranged and processed sequentially.
items.selection_reason: Policy repair kept concise block block_0019.

items.title: 示例：餐厅销售总计
items.review_question: 在餐厅销售总计的例子中，处理器首先从哪个内存地址获取数据？
items.transcript_evidence: For example, let say the processor is totalling up daily sales for a restaurant. It starts by fetching the first transaction from RAM at memory location 100.
items.selection_reason: Policy repair kept concise block block_0020.

items.title: 缓存的速度优势
items.review_question: 缓存能在多少个时钟周期内提供数据？
items.transcript_evidence: Because the cache is so close to the processor, it can typically provide the data in a single clock cycle -- no waiting required. This speeds things up tremendously over having to go back and forth to RAM every single time.
items.selection_reason: Policy repair kept concise block block_0023.

items.title: 缓存命中与未命中
items.review_question: 什么是缓存命中？什么是缓存未命中？
items.transcript_evidence: When data requested in RAM is already stored in the cache like this it’s called a cache hit, and if the data requested isn’t in the cache, so you have to go to RAM, it’s a called a cache miss.
items.selection_reason: Policy repair kept concise block block_0024.
